contract.name: v2/awards/award_id.md
contract.description: Returns the award profile payload for a single award (contract, IDV, or financial assistance) identified by its generated hash or internal id.

award_id.description: Identifier segment inserted into the path; accepts generated unique award hashes (for example `CONT_AWD_...`) or numeric internal award ids.
award_id.constraints: Required path segment; case-sensitive; canonical endpoint expects a trailing slash (requests without it receive 301 redirects); invalid numeric ids return a JSON 404 payload, while malformed slugs return an HTML 404 page.

outputSchema.description: Award profile object combining shared fields with category-specific sections for contracts/IDVs and financial assistance.

generated_unique_award_id.description: Canonical generated award identifier; assistance hashes may be normalized before being returned.

category.description: Award family label; observed values include contract, idv, grant, direct payment, loans, and other.

type.description: Award subtype code such as A, C, IDV_E, 03, 08, 10, or 11.

type_description.description: Human-readable description of the award type.

description.description: Narrative or purpose description derived from the base transaction.

date_signed.description: Base award signature date; can be null for some loan records.

base_exercised_options.description: Total obligated dollars through exercised options; null for assistance awards.

base_and_all_options.description: Ceiling including all options for contracts/IDVs; null for assistance.

total_obligation.description: Cumulative obligated dollars for the award.

total_subaward_amount.description: Sum of subaward spending, null when data is unavailable.

total_account_outlay.description: Sum of outlays across related Treasury accounts (frequently zero in observed payloads).

total_account_obligation.description: Sum of obligations across related Treasury accounts (frequently zero in observed payloads).

total_outlay.description: Additional outlay field present for all categories; observed values were null.

piid.description: Procurement Instrument Identifier for contract and IDV awards.

uri.description: Legacy URI identifier for some assistance awards.

total_subsidy_cost.description: Subtotal specific to loan programs; zero or null in observed responses.

total_loan_value.description: Face value for loan awards; null for other categories.

non_federal_funding.description: Reported non-federal contribution amount for grants and similar assistance.

total_funding.description: Total funding amount for assistance awards.

transaction_obligated_amount.description: Latest transaction obligated amount for assistance awards.

record_type.description: Record type code returned on assistance payloads; absent for contracts and IDVs.

funding_agency.description: Agency providing funding; object includes id, has_agency_page, toptier_agency, subtier_agency, and office_agency_name. Assistance responses may return null.

awarding_agency.description: Agency issuing the award; mirrors funding_agency structure and was always present in observed payloads.

recipient.description: Recipient profile including name, UEI/DUNS identifiers, parent recipient fields, business_categories array, and a nested location object with address components.

period_of_performance.description: Object containing start_date, end_date, last_modified_date, and (for contracts/IDVs) potential_end_date. Assistance awards may supply null start/end dates.

place_of_performance.description: Location object for the place of performance; string fields may be null when unknown.

latest_transaction_contract_data.description: Contract competition metadata (e.g., extent_competed, solicitation_procedures, type_of_contract_pricing_description, product_or_service_code, naics). Present for contract and IDV awards; null for assistance.

parent_award.description: Parent IDV metadata including agency identifiers, PIID, and generated_unique_award_id for child contracts. Null for root IDVs and assistance awards.

psc_hierarchy.description: Product Service Code hierarchy with toptier_code, midtier_code, subtier_code, and base_code leaves. Present for contracts and IDVs.

naics_hierarchy.description: NAICS hierarchy with toptier_code, midtier_code, and base_code leaves. Present for contracts and IDVs.

cfda_info.description: Array of CFDA detail objects for assistance awards (fields include cfda_number, cfda_title, applicant_eligibility, beneficiary_eligibility, and funding amounts).

funding_opportunity.description: Assistance-only object with number and goals keys, both observed as null.

account_outlays_by_defc.description: Array of objects with code and amount fields keyed by Disaster Emergency Fund Code; arrays were empty in observed data.

account_obligations_by_defc.description: Array of objects with code and amount fields showing obligations by DEFC; arrays were empty in observed data.

executive_details.description: Executive compensation summary with an officers array (five entries) containing name and amount fields that are frequently null.

body.type_description: DIRECT PAYMENT WITH UNRESTRICTED USE (RETIREMENT, PENSION, VETERANS BENEFITS, ETC.) (D)

quirks: Requests without the trailing slash receive a 301 redirect to the canonical URL.
Path accepts both generated award hashes and numeric internal identifiers and returns the same payload for each.
Award identifiers are case-sensitive; lower-case hashes miss the route and return the HTML 404 variant.
Financial assistance responses omit contract-specific sections such as latest_transaction_contract_data and the NAICS/PSC hierarchies.
Funding agency can be null for assistance payloads despite documentation marking it required.
Responses include a total_outlay field that is absent from the published contract schema.
Assistance hashes in requests may be normalized (for example, agency suffix adjustments) before being echoed back in generated_unique_award_id.
risks: Clients must branch on category because contracts/IDVs and assistance awards expose different nested structures.
Error payloads vary: numeric-looking misses return JSON, while malformed strings return HTML 404 pages.
Callers that do not follow redirects will fail if they omit the trailing slash.
gaps: Non-empty account_outlays_by_defc and account_obligations_by_defc arrays were not observed, so real-world value magnitudes remain unverified.
Full enumeration of possible award type codes beyond the sampled values is still unknown.
Only simple 404 errors were exercised; other error scenarios (authorization, throttling) remain untested.

probes.notes: Contract lookup via generated unique ID returns contract payload with empty DEFC arrays and populated contract metadata.
Numeric award id resolves to the same contract payload, confirming support for internal identifiers.
IDV payload includes contract metadata while parent_award remains null.
Direct payment award normalizes the hash suffix and returns assistance-specific funding_opportunity.
Loan award exposes total_loan_value and total_subsidy_cost values specific to loan programs.
Grant response shows funding_agency can be null while CFDA information remains populated.
Missing trailing slash triggers a 301 redirect rather than returning data.
Lower-case hash variant misses the route and produces the HTML 404 error page.
Numeric ID that does not exist returns a JSON 404 payload.

mismatches: Published documentation spells the assistance field as `funding_opportunty`, but live responses use `funding_opportunity` with `number` and `goals` keys.
Documentation marks funding_agency as required for assistance responses, yet observed grant payloads return it as null.
Contract schema omits the total_outlay field that is present (null) in live responses across categories.
gaps: Non-empty account_outlays_by_defc and account_obligations_by_defc arrays were not observed, so real-world value magnitudes remain unverified.
Full enumeration of possible award type codes beyond the sampled values is still unknown.
Only simple 404 errors were exercised; other error scenarios (authorization, throttling) remain untested.
risks: Clients must branch on category because contracts/IDVs and assistance awards expose different nested structures.
Error payloads vary: numeric-looking misses return JSON, while malformed strings return HTML 404 pages.
Callers that do not follow redirects will fail if they omit the trailing slash.
